title: गीत गोविन्द — दशावतार अष्टपदी
devanagariText:
॥ श्री गीत गोविन्द — दशावतार अष्टपदी ॥
(श्री जयदेव कृत — प्रलय पयोधि जले)

प्रलयपयोधिजले धृतवानसि वेदं
विहितवहित्रचरित्रमखेदम्।
केशव धृतमीनशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ध्रुवम् ॥

क्षितिरतिविपुलतरे तव तिष्ठति पृष्ठे
धरणिधरणकिणचक्रगरिष्ठे।
केशव धृतकच्छपरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ १॥

वसति दशनशिखरे धरणी तव लग्ना
शशिनि कलङ्ककलेव निमग्ना।
केशव धृतसूकररूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ २॥

तव करकमलवरे नखमद्भुतशृङ्गं
दलितहिरण्यकशिपुतनुभृङ्गम्।
केशव धृतनरहरिरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ३॥

छलयसि विक्रमणे बलिमद्भुतवामन
पदनखनीरजनितजनपावन।
केशव धृतवामनरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ४॥

क्षत्रियरुधिरमये जगदपगतपापं
स्नपयसि पयसि शमितभवतापम्।
केशव धृतभृगुपतिरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ५॥

वितरसि दिक्षु रणे दिक्पतिकमनीयं
दशमुखमौलिबलिं रमणीयम्।
केशव धृतरामशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ६॥

वहसि वपुषि विशदे वसनं जलदाभं
हलहतिभीतिमिलितयमुनाभम्।
केशव धृतहलधररूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ ७॥

निन्दसि यज्ञविधेरहह श्रुतिजातं
सदयहृदयदर्शितपशुघातम्।
केशव धृतबुद्धशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ८॥

म्लेच्छनिवहनिधने कलयसि करवालं
धूमकेतुमिव किमपि करालम्।
केशव धृतकल्किशरीर जय जगदीश हरे॥ ९॥

श्रीजयदेवकवेरिदमुदितमुदारं
श्रृणु सुखदं शुभदं भवसारम्।
केशव धृतदशविधरूप जय जगदीश हरे॥ १०॥

॥ इति श्री जयदेव कृत गीत गोविन्द दशावतार अष्टपदी सम्पूर्णा ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Gita Govinda — Dashavatara Ashtapadi ||
(Shri Jayadeva Krit — Pralaya Payodhi Jale)

Pralaya-payodhi-jale dhritavan-asi Vedam
vihita-vahitra-charitram-akhedam |
Keshava dhrita-meena-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || Dhruvam ||

Kshitir-ati-vipulatare tava tishthati prishthe
dharani-dharana-kina-chakra-garishthe |
Keshava dhrita-kachchhapa-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 1 ||

Vasati dashana-shikhare dharani tava lagna
shashini kalanka-kaleva nimagna |
Keshava dhrita-sukara-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 2 ||

Tava kara-kamala-vare nakham-adbhuta-shringam
dalita-Hiranyakashipu-tanu-bhringam |
Keshava dhrita-Narahari-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 3 ||

Chhalayasi Vikramane Balim-adbhuta-Vamana
pada-nakha-neeraja-nita-jana-pavana |
Keshava dhrita-Vamana-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 4 ||

Kshatriya-rudhira-maye jagad-apagata-papam
snapayasi payasi shamita-bhava-tapam |
Keshava dhrita-Bhrigupati-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 5 ||

Vitarasi dikshu rane dikpati-kamaniyam
Dasha-mukha-mauli-balim ramaniyam |
Keshava dhrita-Rama-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || 6 ||

Vahasi vapushi vishade vasanam jaladabham
hala-hati-bhiti-milita-Yamunabham |
Keshava dhrita-Haladhara-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 7 ||

Nindasi yajna-vidher-ahaha shruti-jatam
sadaya-hridaya-darshita-pashu-ghatam |
Keshava dhrita-Buddha-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || 8 ||

Mlechchha-nivaha-nidhane kalayasi karavalam
Dhuma-ketum-iva kimapi karalam |
Keshava dhrita-Kalki-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare || 9 ||

Shri-Jayadeva-kaver-idam-uditam-udaram
shrinu sukhadam shubhadam bhava-saram |
Keshava dhrita-dasha-vidha-rupa jaya Jagadisha Hare || 10 ||

|| Iti Shri Jayadeva Krit Gita Govinda Dashavatara Ashtapadi Sampurna ||
hindiMeaning:
प्रलय के जल-समुद्र में वेद को धारण किया — नौका-चरित्र विहित, अखेद — हे केशव, मीन शरीर धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥

अत्यन्त विस्तृत पृथ्वी आपकी पीठ पर टिकी है, धरणी-धारण के चिह्न-चक्र से भारी — हे केशव, कच्छप (कूर्म) रूप धारी!॥ १॥

आपके दाँत की नोक पर पृथ्वी विराजमान है, जैसे चन्द्रमा पर कलंक-कला — हे केशव, वराह रूप धारी!॥ २॥

आपके कर-कमल के अद्भुत नख-शृंग से हिरण्यकशिपु का देह-भ्रमर विदीर्ण हुआ — हे केशव, नरहरि रूप धारी!॥ ३॥

बलि को विक्रमण में छलते हैं, अद्भुत वामन! पद-नख से उत्पन्न गंगा-जल से जन पावन — हे केशव, वामन रूप धारी!॥ ४॥

क्षत्रिय-रुधिर में जगत् को पाप-रहित स्नान कराया, भवताप शान्त किया — हे केशव, भृगुपति रूप धारी!॥ ५॥

दिशाओं में रणभूमि में दिक्पालों को प्रिय, रावण के मस्तकों का रमणीय बलि चढ़ाया — हे केशव, राम शरीर धारी!॥ ६॥

शुभ्र शरीर पर नीलवसन धारे, हल के प्रहार से भयभीत यमुना-सदृश — हे केशव, हलधर (बलराम) रूप धारी!॥ ७॥

यज्ञविधि में पशुघात देखकर सदय हृदय से निन्दा की — हे केशव, बुद्ध शरीर धारी!॥ ८॥

म्लेच्छ-समूह के विनाश हेतु धूमकेतु-सदृश कराल तलवार धारी — हे केशव, कल्कि शरीर धारी!॥ ९॥

श्री जयदेव कवि का यह उदार गान सुनो — सुखद, शुभद, भवसार — हे केशव, दशविध रूप धारी, जय जगदीश हरे!॥ १०॥
benefits: Invokes all ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu
Most celebrated Dashavatara hymn in Indian literature
Invokes protection through remembrance of divine avatars
Invokes the blessing of liberation from the cycle of birth and death
Purifies the heart and grants devotional ecstasy
Extremely auspicious for daily recitation and singing
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Lord Vishnu, Gita Govinda - Dashavatara Ashtapadi (गीत गोविन्द — दशावतार अष्टपदी) stands as a sacred prayer. Drawn from Jayadeva - Gita Govinda, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to invoke all ten incarnations of Lord Vishnu and bestow liberation from the cycle of birth and death. It is also said to purify the heart and grant devotional ecstasy. This prayer is traditionally recited on Thursday (Guruvar) and Wednesday (Budhvar) and during Janmashtami and Ekadashi to invoke divine grace. With 10 verses and a reading time of about 6 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Gita Govinda - Dashavatara Ashtapadi in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Gita Govinda Dashavatara Ashtapadi by Jayadeva in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Famous Pralaya Payodhi Jale hymn to Vishnu's ten avatars.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Dashavatara Ashtapadi from Gita Govinda?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dashavatara Ashtapadi (दशावतार अष्टपदी) is the first ashtapadi (eight-couplet song) of Sri Jayadeva's celebrated 12th-century Sanskrit kavya the Gita Govinda. Composed by the poet-saint Jayadeva (court-poet of King Lakshmana Sena of Bengal, c.1170–1245 CE) at his ashram in Kenduli (now Kenduli Mela in Birbhum, Bengal), this ashtapadi enumerates the ten avataras of Bhagavan Vishnu — Matsya, Kurma, Varaha, Narasimha, Vamana, Parashurama (Bhrigupati), Rama, Balarama (Haladhara), Buddha and Kalki — each in a single shloka, followed by the refrain Keshava dhrita-... rupa Jaya Jagadisha Hare. The opening verse Pralaya-payodhi-jale dhritavan-asi Vedam — Keshava dhrita-meena-sharira jaya Jagadisha Hare — is among the most chanted Dashavatara hymns in all Indian devotional literature. (Source: Jayadeva — Gita Govinda, Sarga 1, Ashtapadi 1.)

seoFaqs.question: Who was Jayadeva and what is the Gita Govinda?
seoFaqs.answer: Sri Jayadeva (c. 1170–1245 CE) was a Sanskrit poet-saint of Kenduli village in Bengal, court-poet of King Lakshmana Sena of the Sena dynasty and one of the principal figures of the medieval Vaishnava bhakti renaissance. His magna opera, the Gita Govinda (गीत गोविन्द — Song of Govinda), is a 12-canto Sanskrit kavya in 24 ashtapadis (eight-couplet lyric songs) celebrating the divine love of Sri Krishna and Radha — depicting their union (sambhoga), separation (vipralambha), reconciliation, and ultimate divine union as the supreme allegory of jivatma-paramatma yoga. The poem is foundational for Gaudiya Vaishnava theology of madhurya-rasa (sweet-conjugal devotion), ritually chanted daily at the Jagannatha Puri temple as one of the deva-seva offerings, and inspired entire genres of Indian art (Pahari and Mughal miniatures), music (Odissi, Carnatic ashtapadi-keertanas) and dance (Odissi, Bharatanatyam). (Sources: Gita Govinda; Jayadeva hagiography; Jagannatha Puri tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What are the ten avatars enumerated in the Dashavatara Ashtapadi?
seoFaqs.answer: The ashtapadi enumerates Vishnu's ten avataras one per shloka in chronological order: (1) Matsya (Meena-sharira) — fish-form who saved the Vedas from the pralaya-deluge, sheltering Manu's boat (vahitra); (2) Kurma — tortoise-form who supported Mount Mandara during samudra-manthana; (3) Varaha (Sukara-rupa) — boar-form who lifted earth from cosmic waters on his tusks; (4) Narasimha (Narahari-rupa) — man-lion form who tore Hiranyakashipu with claws; (5) Vamana — dwarf-form who measured the three worlds and humbled Bali; (6) Parashurama (Bhrigupati-rupa) — Bhrigu-clan axe-warrior who exterminated the corrupt kshatriya kings 21 times; (7) Rama (Rama-sharira) — Ayodhya-prince who slew the ten-headed Ravana; (8) Balarama (Haladhara-rupa) — plough-bearing brother of Krishna; (9) Buddha — compassionate-hearted who criticized animal-sacrifice in yajna; (10) Kalki — sword-bearing future avatar who will end Kali-yuga by exterminating mlecchas. (Source: Gita Govinda Sarga 1, Ashtapadi 1.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Jayadeva include Buddha as the ninth avatar of Vishnu?
seoFaqs.answer: Jayadeva's enumeration of Buddha as the ninth Vishnu-avatar reflects the post-Gupta Vaishnava synthesis (8th–12th century CE) which absorbed Buddha into the Dashavatara scheme to integrate the formerly-rival Bauddha tradition into mainstream Hinduism. Verse 8 specifically describes Buddha's role: Nindasi yajna-vidher-ahaha shruti-jatam, sadaya-hridaya-darshita-pashu-ghatam — You criticized the yajna-procedures of the shrutis, having seen with a compassionate heart the slaughter of animals. This frames Buddha not as a refutation of Vedic religion but as a Vishnu-incarnation sent to correct an excess (the pashu-bali / animal-sacrifice abuse of late-Vedic ritualism) and re-establish dharma through ahimsa. The acceptance is consistent with the Bhagavata Purana 1.3.24 and Vishnu Purana 3.18 — though Madhva tradition substitutes Balarama for Buddha and treats Buddha as a moha-avatara (deluding form) rather than a true avatara. (Sources: Bhagavata Purana 1.3.24; Vishnu Purana 3.18; Gita Govinda Ashtapadi 1.)

seoFaqs.question: When is the Dashavatara Ashtapadi traditionally recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Dashavatara Ashtapadi is most powerfully sung on Thursday (Guruvar — Vishnu's day), Wednesday (Budhvar — Krishna's day), Krishna Janmashtami (the central Krishna festival), every Ekadashi, all the avatara-jayanti days (Matsya Jayanti — Chaitra Shukla Tritiya, Narasimha Jayanti — Vaishakha Shukla Chaturdashi, Vamana Jayanti — Bhadrapad Shukla Dvadashi, Rama Navami — Chaitra Shukla Navami, Parashurama Jayanti — Vaishakha Shukla Tritiya, Buddha Purnima — Vaishakha Purnima), at Jagannatha Puri as one of the daily deva-seva offerings, during Rasa-Lila and Holi celebrations in Vrindavan and Mathura, at every Vaishnava bhajan-mandali, in Carnatic and Hindustani concerts as a celebrated ashtapadi-keertana, and during all major Vishnu-temple utsavas. (Sources: Jagannatha Puri paddhati; Vaishnava bhajan tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: How does Jayadeva's Dashavatara Ashtapadi differ from other Dashavatara stotras?
seoFaqs.answer: All celebrate Vishnu's ten avataras but differ in source, language and emphasis. (1) The Dashavatara Stotra of the Gita Govinda (Jayadeva, 12th c.) is a lyrical kavya-quality 10-shloka song with the powerful Keshava dhrita-... rupa Jaya Jagadisha Hare refrain — sung in Carnatic ashtapadi-keertanas, Odissi dance and at Jagannatha Puri; (2) The Dashavatara Stotra of Madhvacharya (13th c.) is a Dvaita-Vedanta theological exposition replacing Buddha with Balarama; (3) The Dashavatara Stotra in the Bhagavata Purana (1.3) lists 22 avataras with Krishna as svayam-bhagavan rather than an avatara; (4) The Dashavatara from Vishnu Purana 3.18 is a foundational scriptural enumeration. Jayadeva's version stands out for its musicality, kavya-beauty, and ritual centrality at Jagannatha Puri, making it the most-recited Dashavatara hymn across Vaishnava traditions. (Sources: Gita Govinda; comparative Dashavatara literature.)
